title: TETRAHYDROCURCUMIN
description: Tetrahydrocurcumin (THC) is a major metabolite of curcumin with enhanced bioavailability, water solubility, and chemical stability compared to its parent compound. It exerts potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and neuroprotective effects by reducing oxidative stress, modulating inflammatory pathways, and protecting mitochondrial function. THC has demonstrated therapeutic potential for cardiovascular health, metabolic dysfunction, neurodegenerative diseases, and age-related conditions.

research.title: Positive Tetrahydrocurcumin-Associated Brain-Related Metabolomic Implications
research.summary: THC demonstrates strong antioxidant properties and effectively reduces amyloid β aggregates in various brain dysfunction models including traumatic brain injury, ischemia-reperfusion injury, Alzheimer's disease, and Parkinson's disease.

research.title: Nutraceutical activation of Sirt1: a review
research.summary: Tetrahydrocurcumin is identified as a nutraceutical that enhances Sirt1 synthesis and protein expression, contributing to anti-aging effects and improved healthspan similar to calorie restriction.

research.title: Tetrahydrocurcumin alleviates hypertension, aortic stiffening and oxidative stress in rats with nitric oxide deficiency
research.summary: THC at 50-100 mg/kg significantly reduced blood pressure, peripheral vascular resistance, and aortic stiffness while improving oxidative stress markers in hypertensive rats.

research.title: Tetrahydrocurcumin-Related Vascular Protection: An Overview of the Findings from Animal Disease Models
research.summary: THC demonstrates preventive effects on vascular dysfunction and hypertension development through improvements in hemodynamic status, aortic elasticity, and reduction of oxidative stress in various animal models.

research.title: The role of tetrahydrocurcumin in disease prevention and treatment
research.summary: Comprehensive review highlighting THC's important roles in prevention and treatment of various diseases, with enhanced bioavailability compared to curcumin from herbal medicine and dietary sources.

research.title: Tetrahydrocurcumin Ameliorates Skin Inflammation by Modulating Autophagy in High-Fat Diet-Induced Obese Mice
research.summary: THC demonstrated anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects by modulating autophagy processes in skin tissue of obese mice, addressing obesity-induced chronic low-grade inflammation.

research.title: Tetrahydrocurcumin Enhances Islet Cell Function and Attenuates Apoptosis in Mouse Islets
research.summary: THC treatment enhanced islet cell function and reduced apoptosis before transplantation, demonstrating antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that protect against ischemic damage.

research.title: Tetrahydrocurcumin: effect on chloroquine-mediated oxidative damage in rat kidney
research.summary: Oral administration of THC at 80 mg/kg significantly prevented chloroquine-induced nephrotoxicity by decreasing lipid peroxidation and increasing antioxidant levels in kidney tissue.

research.title: Efficacy of TurmiZn, a Metallic Complex of Curcuminoids-Tetrahydrocurcumin and Zinc on Bioavailability, Antioxidant, and Cytokine Modulation Capability
research.summary: A curcuminoid-THC-zinc complex showed 2-3 fold increase in bioavailability compared to curcumin and THC alone, with enhanced antioxidant capability and inflammatory cytokine inhibition.

sentiment.summary: User feedback discussions show limited direct experience with tetrahydrocurcumin specifically, with most users discussing curcumin or turmeric. Users acknowledge THC's superior bioavailability compared to regular curcumin, but there is minimal anecdotal evidence about its standalone effects or benefits.

effects.label: Enhanced Bioavailability:
effects.description: Demonstrated 2-3 fold better absorption compared to standard curcumin in multiple studies

effects.label: Dose-Dependent Effects:
effects.description: Both upregulation and downregulation of certain enzymes depends on concentration used

effects.label: Superior Stability:
effects.description: Greater water solubility and chemical stability compared to curcumin while maintaining therapeutic benefits

effects.label: Metabolite Advantage:
effects.description: Acts as an active metabolite of curcumin with similar or enhanced biological activities

dosage.label: Animal Model Range:
dosage.description: Studies used 50-100 mg/kg body weight with significant therapeutic effects observed

dosage.label: Human Supplement Form:
dosage.description: Often combined with curcumin in formulations or metal complexes for enhanced delivery

dosage.label: Concentration Effects:
dosage.description: 40µM showed limited proliferation effects in cell studies, while 80 mg/kg was effective for nephroprotection

dosage.label: Duration:
dosage.description: Benefits observed with both short-term (2-5 weeks) and longer-term administration in animal studies

sideEffects.label: Trace Mineral Depletion:
sideEffects.description: Potential for zinc, copper, and selenium depletion with long-term curcuminoid use based on NAC-related reports

sideEffects.label: Digestive Interactions:
sideEffects.description: Can affect pepsin function and enzyme activity in concentration-dependent manner

sideEffects.label: Limited Human Data:
sideEffects.description: Most safety profiles derived from animal studies with minimal long-term human clinical data

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: No major adverse effects reported in animal studies at therapeutic doses

availability.label: Supplement Forms:
availability.description: Available as standalone ingredient or combined with curcumin, often with bioavailability enhancers like piperine or metal chelates

availability.label: Research Focus:
availability.description: More commonly studied in research settings than widely marketed as standalone consumer supplement compared to curcumin
